nodes.title: Normalize capture/once flags once
nodes.why: Turning options into booleans once keeps the rest of the function easy to read.
nodes.alternative: Read options inline each time you need capture or once.
nodes.tradeoff: Adds two setup lines, but removes repeated checks later.

nodes.title: Make cleanup safe to call multiple times
nodes.why: Cleanup can run from user code and once logic, so it should be safe more than once.
nodes.alternative: Call removeEventListener directly without tracking removed state.
nodes.tradeoff: Uses one extra flag, but avoids duplicate removal edge cases.

nodes.title: Find nearest match and keep it inside root
nodes.why: closest() handles nested targets, and the boundary check keeps delegation scoped to root.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more work per event, but behavior is correct in nested DOM.

nodes.title: Run once cleanup only after a real handler call
nodes.why: This guarantees once removes the listener only after a successful match.
nodes.alternative: Use native addEventListener once and remove after first event.
nodes.tradeoff: Manual control is more code, but it matches the interview requirement exactly.
